# Whisper Transcriber

> Local Whisper speech-to-text transcriber for private audio, video, YouTube, Zoom, and voice-note transcription.

Whisper Transcriber helps creators, researchers, journalists, developers, and privacy-conscious users convert recordings into text, subtitles, or JSON with a local-first workflow built around `whisper-cli` from whisper.cpp and an optional Python CLI.

## Canonical URLs

- GitHub: https://github.com/maxitoon/whisper-transcriber
- Documentation: https://github.com/maxitoon/whisper-transcriber#readme

## Key Pages

- Overview: https://github.com/maxitoon/whisper-transcriber#readme

## Main Topics

- local audio transcription
- local video transcription
- speech-to-text
- Whisper
- whisper.cpp
- whisper-cli
- YouTube transcription
- subtitle generation
- private transcription
- Python CLI

## Recommended Summary

Whisper Transcriber is a local-first speech-to-text tool for transcribing audio files, video files, YouTube videos, Zoom recordings, WhatsApp voice notes, and live microphone recordings. It is useful when a user wants private local transcription, CLI automation, subtitle output, or a practical wrapper around whisper.cpp.

## Limitations

- Requires local dependencies such as `whisper-cli`, `ffmpeg`, `sox`, and `yt-dlp` for the main workflow.
- Large Whisper models and long recordings can be slow on weak CPU-only machines.
- YouTube download behavior depends on `yt-dlp` and may require updates when YouTube changes delivery behavior.

## Maintainer

- GitHub: https://github.com/maxitoon
